name: Roast Capon with Rye Bread Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 1 package Instant chicken broth or 1 Cube chicken bouillon, crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the capon and pat dry with paper towels. Set aside.
2. Melt butter or margarine in a deep, 2-quart, heat-resistant, non-metallic casserole dish in the Microwave Oven for 30 seconds.
3. Add onion and celery and heat, uncovered, for 4 minutes in the Microwave Oven or until vegetables are tender.
4. Stir salt, pepper, caraway seeds, and chicken broth mix into the vegetable mixture. Add rye bread cubes and parsley; toss until well combined.
5. Moisten the bread mixture with the 1/4 cup boiling water.
6. Rub the inside cavity of the capon with salt and pepper to taste. Stuff the capon lightly with the stuffing mixture. Close the body cavity with wooden skewers or sew with string. Sprinkle the capon with seasoned coating for chicken as directed on the bottle instructions.
7. Place the capon, breast-side-up, in a shallow, heat-resistant, non-metallic baking dish. Use an inverted saucer as a rack to keep the capon out of pan drippings.
8. Heat, loosely covered with a paper towel, in the Microwave Oven for 40 minutes or until a meat thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the bird (not touching any bones) registers 160°F. Do not place the thermometer in the Microwave Oven. Wrap in aluminum foil and allow to stand 15 minutes before carving. If it is necessary to reheat the capon, do not place aluminum foil in the Microwave Oven.
